ocr.verbatim:
NAME : DATE : 27/03/2024
AGE : 81 YRS ♀
CATH ID : 2401/03/2026
SEX : FEMALE
IP NO : AHH-1792/03/26
CORONARY ANGIOPLASTY REPORT - (PTCA)
Route : RIGHT RADIAL ARTERY
Procedure : PTCA TO LCX
Access : 7-F SHEATH
RFA : ,130/80 mmhg.
INJ Heparin : 10,000 IU
Guiding Catheter : 6F EBU 3.0
Coronary wire : Sion Blue
Pre-dilatation : Sapphire 2.5 X 12 mm. balloon at 12 atm.
Stent : LCX- GRACE 2.75 X 36 mm at 11 atm. [DES]
Post- dilatation : Sapphire nc 3.0 x 10 mm balloon at 18 atm.
Post Ptca Act : 290
Final result : Excellent final result, no haze / dissection / Thrombus TIMI-III Flow achieved
Residual stenosis 0%
LCX: Using 6F EBU 3.0 catheter, Left coronary Ostium was engaged and 0.014 Sion Blue ns wire was advanced in the distal LCX. The lesion was dilated with Sapphire 2.5 X 12 mm.balloon at 12 atm, and stented with LCX- GRACE 2.75 X 36 mm at 11 atm, Post-dilatation done with Sapphire nc 3.0 x 10 mm balloon at 18 atm. with an excellent final result.
PTCA TO LCX WITH SINGLE STENT DONE SUCCESSFULLY
LONG TERM DUAL ANTIPLATELETS.
Note: All stents having inherent risk of Re-stenosis and thrombosis. Please Continue ant platelet drugs regularly. Do not stop medicines without Consulting Physician or Cardiologist.
MD (MBBS), DM (Cardiology)
Interventional Cardiologist
सुचना * पुडील बेलेस येताना हा कागद सोबत आणावा. * औषधींची अंतर्ज्ञान अस्त्यास प्रथम सांगणे. * औषधे डॉक्टरांना दाखवूनच घ्या.
Phone :

records.summary: This is a typed report of a successful PTCA (Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty) procedure performed on the left circumflex artery (LCX) using a single drug-eluting stent. The procedure involved pre-dilatation, stenting, and post-dilatation, resulting in an excellent final outcome with no dissection, thrombus, or residual stenosis.
key_observations: Procedure: PTCA to LCX via right radial artery
Stent used: LCX-GRACE 2.75 X 36 mm [DES]
Final result: Excellent, TIMI-III flow, 0% residual stenosis
Post-procedure instructions: Long-term dual antiplatelets required
Note: Patient must continue antplatelet drugs and consult physician before stopping them

records.stage_evidence: Stent deployed in LAD, handwritten note 'PTCA To LAD - Single Stent Done'

records.summary: This is a typed Coronary Angiography Report for an 81-year-old female patient, detailing stenosis findings in the left coronary arteries and recommending PTCA to the LCX. The procedure was performed via RT-Radial route on March 21, 2026.
key_observations: Patient is 81 years old, female, cath ID 2380/03/2026, procedure date 21/03/2026.
Left Anterior Descending (LAD) shows 40% stenosis in mid segment and 30% stenosis in diagonal branch ostium.
Left Circumflex (LCX) shows 80% stenosis in proximal to mid segment; OM branch is normal.
Right Coronary Artery (RCA) is dominant and shows normal vessels including PDA and PLV branches.
Final impression is SVD (Single Vessel Disease) and recommendation is PTCA to LCX.
Report signed by an Interventional Cardiologist.

records.stage_evidence: Handwritten annotations indicate 'PTCA to LCx' (percutaneous transluminal coronary angioplasty to left circumflex artery), suggesting intervention was performed.
